messages.content:
﻿
Computational tasks that
require working with a large set of numbers are best done using a(n)
A. basic calculator
B. adding machine
C. computer
C. computer

﻿
To test a user-defined block of code in the Snap Scripts window, the coder needs to:
A. embed it in a larger program.
B. link it to "when green flag is clicked".
C. click on the grey block.
B. link it to "when green flag is clicked".

﻿
If the daily sales data on items from a store were held
in a list, the item most sold would be the
value
A. minimum
B. maximum
C. average
of that list.
B. maximum

﻿
In order to output the index of a value in a list, make the linear search block a _____in Snap.
A. Command
B. Reporter
C. Predicate
B. Reporter

﻿
A search algorithm that proceeds from the beginning of a list to the end of a list examining each
compartment is called a search.
A. linear
B. binary
C. selection
A. linear

﻿
The binary search algorithm works on the principle of:
A. repeatedly splitting the list and sub-lists in half.
B. examining items in a list in random groups of 2.
C. examining each item in the list.
A. repeatedly splitting the list and sub-lists in half.

﻿
A binary search algorithm is
best coded with a
A. repeat/until
B. repeat
C. for
loop.
A. repeat/until

﻿
In order to swap variable values in computer science, we need to use
A. a repeat loop
B. an if/else command
C. a third variable
D. switch command
C. a third variable

﻿
The time it takes to write code in Snap can be shortened using the
A. repeat
C. paste
option.
B. define
D. duplicate
D. duplicate

﻿
In computer science, placing the items in a list in a certain order is referred to as
the list.
A. searching
B. sorting
C. ordering
D. aligning
B. sorting

﻿
Which sort algorithm continuously swaps 2 values throughout a single iteration until the largest number is at the top?
A. Selection sort.
B. Bubble sort.
C. Merge sort.
D. Insertion sort.
B. Bubble sort.

﻿
If item i of list > item i+1 of list, the first step in swapping the values is to:
A. replace item i of list with item i+1 of list.
B. set temp to item i of list.
C. replace item i+1 of list with temp.
B. set temp to item i of list.

﻿
Bubble sort is descriptive as the name of this sorting algorithm because:
A. values are compared in groups called bubbles.
B. the largest values "bubble" to the top of the list.
C. the last name of the developer is Bubble.
B. the largest values "bubble" to the top of the list.

﻿
Which type of compression
can be reversed on the receiving computer to restore the original document?
A. Lossless
C. Precision
B. Lossy
D. Compact
A. Lossless

﻿
An illustration of data compression might be:
A. writing out a dictation.
B. printing your name.
C. taking class notes.
D. making a Xerox copy.
D. making a Xerox copy.

﻿
An illustration of lossless data compression in classroom note taking might be:
A. writing in shorthand.
C. recording interesting facts.
B. copying every other word.
D. listing main points.
A. writing in shorthand.

﻿
There are
broad
categories of data compression techniques.
A. 1
C. 2
B. 3
D. 4

﻿
The
data
compression technique can produce high degrees of compression.
A. lossless
C. lossy
B. RLE
D. dictionary
C. lossy

﻿
Data files can be fully reconstructed using the
A. precision
C. compact
compression
technique.
B. lossless
D. lossy
B. lossless

﻿
"Compress this sentence" can be compressed to using a vowel removing algorithm.
A. Cmprss ths sntnc
B. Cpress ths sent
C. Cpss ts sntc
D. Cps ths sntce
A. Cmprss ths sntnc

﻿
Storing a sentence in a variable in Snap is equivalent to declaring that variable to be of type
A. char
B. int
C. string
D. float
C. string
